goals.description: Submit the reading response draft by 23:30 to sever the suffocating tension with Roommate_R and reclaim my personal space.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value screams that dragging out this interaction is actively harming our dynamic. I can't let them see me crumbling; I need to hit 'send' and walk away before they notice I'm struggling. If they complain about the timing, I'll just say it's urgent for both of us.

goals.description: Defer cleaning the room entirely until tomorrow morning; negotiate a new schedule directly with Roommate_R if pressed.
goals.rationale: I don't have the physical stamina to scrub anything tonight, especially not when I'm trying to escape this stale environment. Renegotiating the cleaning deadline directly with Roommate_R later is better than wasting energy on a chore I hate. If they push back, I'll say my deadline is tight and I need their support to make it work, not their complaints about the smell.

goals.description: Finish the reading response draft now so I can ditch work mode and actually hang out with cohort_3 without feeling guilty.
goals.rationale: My relationships (0.5 weight) are way more important than this soft-deadline task. Finishing it lets me bond with my close friends, which is what matters most to me right now. The stress of dragging down the group's morale by being messy is real, but finishing *this* specific thing clears the path to that connection.

goals.description: Grab a quiet spot in the library for 90 minutes to crush the writing block before roommate_R gets back at 7 PM.
goals.rationale: I need to get this done fast because the smell is killing my focus, and I don't have the stamina to drag it out. Moving to the library fixes the distraction immediately. goals.description: Do a quick visual sweep of the room (just toss clothes into a hamper, don't fold or scrub) to lower the visible mess level before they walk in.
goals.rationale: This feels like too much effort for now given how groggy I am. I'm prioritizing the social stuff over deep cleaning. If I do a little tidying later, great, but right now my energy is better spent on the reading response and then just chilling with my crew. I can renegotiate the 'cleaning' part directly when they get back if needed.
